lede: A look at November 28 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Jackson Yee and Tyler Glenn, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Ethiopian National Defense Force
events.desc: Over seven hundred civilians are massacred by the Ethiopian National Defense Force and Eritrean Army in Aksum, Ethiopia.

events.title: LaMia Flight 2933
events.desc: LaMia Flight 2933 crashes near Medellín, Colombia, killing 71 of 77 people on board, including members of the Brazilian football club Chapecoense

events.title: South African Airways Flight 295
events.desc: South African Airways Flight 295 crashes into the Indian Ocean, killing all 159 people on board.

events.title: Mount Erebus disaster
events.desc: Air New Zealand Flight 901, a DC-10 sightseeing flight over Antarctica, crashes into Mount Erebus, killing all 257 people on board.

events.title: Michel Micombero
events.desc: Michel Micombero overthrows the monarchy of Burundi and makes himself the first president.

events.title: Grand Ole Opry
events.desc: The Grand Ole Opry begins broadcasting in Nashville, Tennessee, as the WSM Barn Dance.

events.title: Chicago Times-Herald race
events.desc: The first American automobile race takes place over the 54 miles from Chicago's Jackson Park to Evanston, Illinois. Frank Duryea wins in approximately 10 hours.

events.title: Serbo-Bulgarian War
events.desc: Bulgarian victory in the Serbo-Bulgarian War preserves the Unification of Bulgaria.

events.title: Ludwig van Beethoven
events.desc: Beethoven's Piano Concerto No. 5 in E-flat major, Op. 73, premieres at the Gewandhaus in Leipzig.

deaths.text: Jean-Louis Roux, Canadian actor and politician, 34th Lieutenant Governor of Quebec
Kerry Wendell Thornley, American soldier and author
Giovanni Paolo Colonna, Italian organist, composer, and educator

observances.text: Independence Day, celebrating the independence of Albania from Turkey in 1912, the first Albanian flag raise by Skanderbeg in 1443, and for the new parliamentary constitution in 1998.
Bedfordshire day is celebrated in the county of Bedfordshire to celebrate the birth of John Bunyan
Christian feast day: Acacius, Hirenarchus, and companions, of Sebaste
Christian feast day: Feast of the Holy Sovereigns (Episcopal Diocese of Hawaii)
Christian feast day: Herman of Alaska, the anniversary of his actual death. Eastern Orthodox
Christian feast day: James of the Marches
